name: Mimicry Process
description: Mimicry Process in Animals. This process represents a higher-level biological mechanism distinct from molecular/biochemical processes covered in GLMP.
mermaid:
graph TD
    A27[Model Species] --> B27[Appearance Observation]
    C27[Mimic Species] --> B27
    B27 --> D27[Evolutionary Pressure]
    D27 --> E27[Phenotypic Variation]
    E27 --> F27{Successful Mimicry?}
    F27 -->|Yes| G27[Selection Advantage]
    F27 -->|No| H27[Predation Risk]
    G27 --> I27[Mimicry Refinement]
    H27 --> J27[Continued Selection]
    I27 --> K27[Final Mimicry Pattern]
    J27 --> E27
    
    style A27 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style C27 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style B27 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000
    style D27 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style E27 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000
    style F27 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style G27 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style H27 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style I27 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style J27 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style K27 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Programming Framework: A Universal Process Visualization Methodology
sources.notes: Higher-level biological process visualization: Mimicry Process. Created using the Programming Framework methodology for organismal and ecological process analysis.

sources.title: Mimicry: The Evolutionary Biology of a Phenotype
sources.authors: Mappes, J.; Van Grouw, L.A.; Van Someren, J.A.M.

sources.title: The adaptive radiation of Batesian mimicry
Mimicry: The evolutionary dynamics of an ecological interaction
sources.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A

notes: Higher-level biological process. GLMP covers biochemical/molecular processes; this database covers organismal/ecological processes.
